title: Manage resources automatically using resource handles and RAII
text: Prefer RAII objects so resource acquisition and release stay paired across normal and exceptional control flow.
summary: Avoid leaks and manual cleanup complexity by preferring RAII-based resource management.

pack_id: cpp_core
language_id: cpp
section: R
title: Manage resources automatically using resource handles and RAII

reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: malloc_free, manual_lock_unlock, direct_system_call

title: Avoid calling new and delete explicitly
text: The result of new should immediately move into an owning abstraction instead of remaining in a raw pointer.
summary: Explicit new/delete makes ownership and cleanup error-prone.

title: Immediately give the result of an explicit resource allocation to a manager object
text: If you keep an explicitly allocated resource in an unmanaged state, exceptions or returns can leak it.
summary: Raw allocations should be handed off to a manager immediately to avoid leaks on early exit.
